message.content: acquaint

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	_No cycle currently in progress._
21	
22	## Todo
23	
24	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
25	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
26	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
27	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
28	
29	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
30	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
31	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
32	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
33	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
34	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
35	 is the read-only check.
36	
37	1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
38	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
39	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
40	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
41	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
42	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
43	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
44	   (`init.rs`, tests).
45	
46	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
47	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
48	   issue.
49	2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
50	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
51	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
52	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
53	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
54	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
55	3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
56	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
57	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
58	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
59	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
60	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
61	4. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
62	   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
63	   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
64	   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
65	   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
66	   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
67	   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
68	     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
69	     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
70	     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
71	     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
72	   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
73	     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
74	   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
75	     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
76	     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
77	     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
78	     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
79	     sub-step in the merge.
80	   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
81	   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
82	     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
83	5. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
84	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
85	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
86	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
87	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
88	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
89	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
90	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
91	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
92	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
93	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
94	6. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
95	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
96	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
97	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
98	   the felt bottleneck.
99	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
100	7. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
101	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
102	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
103	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
104	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
105	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
106	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
107	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
108	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
109	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
110	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
111	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
112	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
113	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
114	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
115	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
116	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
117	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
118	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
119	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
120	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
121	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
122	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
123	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
124	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
125	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
126	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
127	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
128	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
129	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
130	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
131	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
132	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
133	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
134	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
135	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
136	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
137	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
138	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
139	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
140	12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
141	    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
142	    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
143	    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
144	    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
145	13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
146	14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
147	    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
148	    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
149	    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
150	    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
151	    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
152	15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
153	    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
154	    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
155	    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
156	16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
157	    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
158	    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
159	    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
160	    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
161	    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
162	17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
163	    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
164	    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
165	    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
166	    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
167	18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
168	    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
169	    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
170	    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
171	    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
172	19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
173	    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
174	    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
175	    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
176	    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
177	    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
178	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
179	20. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
180	    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
181	    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
182	    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
183	    post-init commands can. Depends on the
184	    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
185	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
186	21. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
187	    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
188	    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
189	    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
190	    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
191	    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
192	    visual inconsistency.
193	22. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
194	    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
195	    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
196	    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
197	    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
198	23. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
199	    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
200	    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
201	    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
202	    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
203	    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
204	    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
205	    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
206	    sweep.
207	24. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
208	    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
209	    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
210	    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
211	25. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
212	    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
213	    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
214	    2026-04-24).
215	26. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
216	    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
217	    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
218	    place first. [[12]]
219	27. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
220	    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
221	    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
222	28. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
223	    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
224	    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
225	29. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
226	    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
227	    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
228	    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
229	    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
230	    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
231	    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
232	    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
233	    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
234	30. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
235	    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
236	    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
237	31. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
238	    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
239	    differs from entry. [[20]]
240	32. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
241	    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
242	    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
243	33. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
244	    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
245	    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
246	    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
247	    needed for the new layout.
248	34. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
249	    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
250	    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
251	    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
252	    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
253	35. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
254	36. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
255	37. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
256	38. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
257	39. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
258	40. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
259	41. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
260	42. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
261	43. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
262	44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
263	    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
264	    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
265	    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
266	    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
267	    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
268	    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
269	    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
270	    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
271	    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
272	    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
273	    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
274	    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
275	    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
276	    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
277	    it adds value over the resolution chain.
278	45. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
279	    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
280	    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
281	    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
282	    lines / typos surface.
283	
284	## Bugs
285	
286	   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
287	   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
288	   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
289	   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).
290	
291	1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
292	   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
293	   four gaps:
294	   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
295	     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
296	     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
297	     are never surfaced.
298	   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
299	     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
300	     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
301	     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
302	     printed twice.
303	   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
304	     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
305	     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
306	     on write would close this.
307	   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
308	     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
309	     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
310	     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
311	     where there may be no next run.
312	
313	   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
314	   patches one related case — the detached child eating
315	   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
316	   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
317	   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
318	   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.
319	
320	## Done
321	
322	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
323	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
324	
325	_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._
326	
327	- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
328	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
329	- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
330	- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
331	- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
332	- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]
333	- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[33]]
334	
335	# References
336

338	[2]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-close-sb_ide-elimination-0520
339	[3]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-todo-renumber--fix-todopy-0530

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
 is the read-only check.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[4]]
4. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
     sub-step in the merge.
   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
5. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
6. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
7. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
20. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
21. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
22. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
23. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
24. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
25. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
26. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
27. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
28. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
29. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
30. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
31. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[20]]
32. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
33. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
34. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
35. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
36. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
37. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
38. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
39. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
40. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
41. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
42. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
43. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.
45. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
    lines / typos surface.

## Bugs

   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[33]]

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	_No cycle currently in progress._
21	
22	## Priorities
23	
24	#2. single-field
25	#5. por -> dual
26	#7. por/duality parity
27	
28	## Todo
29	
30	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
31	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
32	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
33	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
34	
35	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
36	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
37	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
38	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
39	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
40	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
41	 is the read-only check.
42	
43	1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
44	   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
45	   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
46	   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
47	   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
48	   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
49	   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
50	     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
51	     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
52	     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
53	     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
54	   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
55	     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
56	   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
57	     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
58	     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
59	     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
60	     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
61	     sub-step in the merge.
62	   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
63	   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
64	     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
65	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
66	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
67	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
68	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
69	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
70	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
71	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
72	   (`init.rs`, tests).
73	
74	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
75	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
76	   issue.
77	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
78	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
79	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
80	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
81	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
82	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
83	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
84	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
85	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
86	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
87	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
88	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
89	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
90	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
91	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
92	   the felt bottleneck.
93	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
94	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
95	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
96	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
97	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
98	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Priorities

#2. single-field
#5. por -> dual
#7. por/duality parity

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
 is the read-only check.

1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
     sub-step in the merge.
   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**

lastPrompt: Hmm, to indicate the priorities I wanted, but reordering the list is wrong since there are references. Ok, I added ## Priorities, is that better. Maybe you'd suggest a better way?

content.text:
`## Priorities` works but it's fragile in a specific way: the entry numbers it cites (`#2`, `#5`, `#7`) aren't stable — the file's own intro says `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` renumbers freely on insert/reorder. The moment you add an entry above #2 or run fix-todo, the priority list silently points at the wrong items.

Two tighter alternatives:

1. **Inline priority marker on the entry itself** — prefix the bold title with `[P0]` / `[P1]` (or `🔴` / `🟡` if you want emoji). Intent lives with the item; survives any renumber; one source of truth. `## Priorities` then disappears, and `validate-todo` could later sort-check that markers come first.

   ```
   2. **[P0] single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.** …
   ```

2. **Keep `## Priorities`, but cite by title not number.** Titles are far more stable than position; readable to a human; survives fix-todo. Costs one line of duplication per priority entry.

   ```
   ## Priorities
   - single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field
   - `por → dual` conversion
   - `por/dual` parity + `dual → por` conversion
   ```

I'd lean toward **(1)** — single source of truth, no duplication, naturally extensible to `[P1]`/`[P2]` tiers, and clear from any view of the entry that it's prioritized. The cost is a small convention to remember; the gain is that you can never end up with a stale priorities list.

message.content: I like 2, as you don't have to scan/search and the edit it localized as we'll as I can add "new things", like chores-12, also easily and localized. Push back if you disagree

content.text:
Agreed — the extensibility point flips it. Inline markers can only tier `## Todo` entries; a `## Priorities` list by title can also point at chores sections, bugs, design notes, or anything else that's "important right now," which is a different (and more useful) concept than P0/P1 tiers on Todo alone.

One small thing to be aware of: titles can drift if you rename a `## Todo` entry's bold title and forget to update `## Priorities`. Low risk in practice (titles are stable, and you'd notice), and `vc-x1 validate-todo` could later add an exact-match check ("every `## Priorities` line that isn't a chores/bugs ref matches some Todo entry's title") to make it tooling-enforced.

Want me to clean up the current `## Priorities` content — rewrite the three lines to match the actual entry titles (the current `#2 single-field`, `#5 por -> dual`, `#7 por/duality parity` are abbreviated and the numbers will go stale)?

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Priorities

P1: chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md
P2: **single-field.. **
P3: **por -> dual.. ** & **por/duality parity.. **

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so

content.text:
Both ideas land well. Three small refinements:

1. **Two dots vs three.** `..` reads like a Rust range or a typo. The standard ellipsis is `...` (three dots, easy to type) or `…` (single char, matches the em-dashes used elsewhere). Pick one and stick with it — I'd lean `...` for typeability.

2. **Trailing space inside the bold.** `**single-field.. **` has a space before the closing `**`. Cleaner as `**single-field...**`. Markdown tolerates the space but it's slightly off.

3. **Multiple items at the same tier — one per line.** Currently `P3: **por -> dual...** & **por/duality parity...**` joins two with `&`. Splitting to two `P3:` lines is more grep-friendly (`grep '^P3:'` gives you all of them) and reads the same whether a tier has 1 or 5 items:

   ```
   P3: **por -> dual...**
   P3: **por/duality parity...**
   ```

Worth adding a one-line convention note above the section so the rules are self-describing:

```
## Priorities

 Prefix-match `## Todo` titles with `...` (e.g. `**single-field...**` →
 unique starts-with). Lines that don't prefix-match are free-form
 (chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders). `Pn:` tiers group equal-priority
 items; one item per line.

P1: chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md
P2: **single-field...**
P3: **por -> dual...**
P3: **por/duality parity...**
```

The semantics ("tier vs strict rank") get pinned down too — `Pn:` is a tier, multiple lines OK. And `vc-x1 validate-todo` could later enforce: any line whose body looks like `**...**` prefix-matches exactly one `## Todo` title.

One question for the spec: should P1 be the **highest** priority (current draft) or **lowest** (some teams use P1=urgent, others P0=urgent)? Worth nailing in the convention note so it doesn't flip later.

content.text:
Three things caught my eye:

1. **Bullet 1 trails off.** "`Pn:` tiers group equal-priority" — needs an object: "...group equal-priority **entries**." Also, the `P1 = highest` note got lost — a reader can't tell if P0 or P1 is the top tier. Add it.

2. **Bullet 2 grammar.** "lines with `...` **is** a prefix-match" — plural subject, should be **are**. And a line doesn't *is* a prefix-match — it *uses* one. Suggest: "a trailing `...` marks a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title (must be unique)".

3. **Bullet 3 double space** between "without" and "a".

Also worth noting (not a bug, a good choice): wrapping the markers in backticks (`` `**single-field...**` ``) renders them as inline code instead of bold, which is what you want — the `**` are *markers* signaling "match this `## Todo` title prefix," not emphasis. If they were actually bolded, the asterisks would vanish in the rendered view and the prefix-match convention would be invisible. The convention note should mention this so it doesn't drift back to plain bold later.

Suggested fix:

```
## Priorities

- Tiered list, highest priority first. `Pn:` groups
  equal-priority entries (P1 is highest); one entry per line.
- A body of `` `**prefix...**` `` (inline-code preserves the
  `**` markers) is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title —
  must be unique.
- Entries without a `...` prefix-match are free-form
  (chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders).
```

Want me to apply this, or do you want to keep iterating the wording yourself?

message.content: I've tweaked differently, but there is another even more immediate problem. todo.md is too large and you're using 1000+ tokens to read the it and you took 30+ seconds, that's a waste of energy. I'm thinking we need to split todo.md refactoring with less important maybe todo.other.md, thoughts?

content.text:
Two things at once — and the cheaper fix is probably on my side.

**My fault first.** I read the *whole* file when I needed `## In Progress` + `## Priorities` — maybe 30 lines. Read with `offset`/`limit` would have cost a fraction. CLAUDE.md could codify: "to check current focus, read `notes/todo.md` lines 1–50 only; load the rest only when picking up an item." That alone reclaims most of the cost without touching the file structure.

**On the split itself — pushback on the "important vs other" axis.** Two problems:

- **"Important" is fuzzy and migrates.** Today's #3 becomes tomorrow's `todo.other` entry, then back again. Items churn between files as Priorities shifts. Whereas the *current* mechanism — `## Priorities` referencing entries in `## Todo` — already answers "what's important" without moving anything.
- **Numbering domain splits.** `#3` in `todo.md` vs `#3` in `todo.other.md` collide; conversational refs ("let's do #3") need a file qualifier; `fix-todo` has to renumber across both.

**Cleaner split — by lifecycle state, not importance.** Keep `todo.md` as the hot, frequently-changing surface (everything that gets touched per cycle); move the cold backlog to its own file:

- `todo.md` → intro, `## In Progress`, `## Priorities`, `## Bugs`. ~60 lines. Loaded every session.
- `todo-backlog.md` (or `backlog.md`) → the long `## Todo` list. Read only when picking up an item, or via grep.
- `done.md` already follows this pattern.

Why this works:
- The split matches access frequency, not subjective importance.
- Priorities still references backlog entries by `**prefix...**` — no semantic change, just a different file resolves the prefix.
- Numbering stays a single domain (one Todo list, lives in one file).
- `fix-todo` / `validate-todo` get one new file path; small change.

My honest take: **start with the bot-reads-less fix** (cheap, behavioral, zero churn). If the file keeps growing past ~500 lines, then do the lifecycle split. Splitting prematurely adds tooling and cross-file friction for a problem that may not need it yet.

file.content:
## Working Directory

Prefer staying in the project root. Use `-R` flags or absolute paths
to target other directories rather than `cd`. If `cd` seems necessary,
discuss with the user first — losing track of cwd causes subtle
command failures downstream.

When invoking shell commands, prefer the shortest unambiguous path —
usually relative to cwd (`ls notes/`, not
`ls /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/`). Long absolute paths
clutter the transcript without adding information. Out-of-workspace
paths (`/tmp/...`, `~/.config/...`) stay absolute. Read/Edit/Write
tool args also stay absolute — that's a tool-boundary constraint,
not a stylistic choice.

## Memory

Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
CLAUDE.md it provides no capability CLAUDE.md doesn't already cover,
and it loses on discoverability:

- **CLAUDE.md** — at the repo root, a well-known location for bot
  instructions, committed, reviewable, visible to every collaborator
  (human or bot).
- **Memory directory** — hidden under the user's home, tied to one
  machine, invisible to anyone but the bot, never diffed or reviewed.

Easy for everyone to find beats convenient for the bot alone. Put
durable context in CLAUDE.md (or committed `notes/`) instead.

## Speculation marker

Durable text the bot writes — CLAUDE.md, `notes/`, commit bodies,
chores sections — should stick to observations and direct descriptions
of the code or data. If a mechanism, hypothesis, or causal claim
enters the text, prefix it with "The bot thinks ..." so a reader can
tell the measured from the inferred.

**Why:** unmarked speculation reads like evidence, and a future reader
(or the bot on a later session) can pick it up as a known fact when
it's not. Measured / inferred is a distinction worth keeping visible
in the written record.

**How to apply:** observations and factual descriptions need no
marker. Prefix with "The bot thinks ..." (or a close variant like
"The bot's guess is ...") when the claim is a mechanism ("X wins
because Y caches better"), a cause ("the drift was due to thermal
state"), a prediction ("this should scale linearly"), or any
reasoning not directly supported by the data on hand.

input.new_string:
paths (`/tmp/...`, `~/.config/...`) stay absolute. Read/Edit/Write
tool args also stay absolute — that's a tool-boundary constraint,
not a stylistic choice.

## File reads — read the slice you need

Long notes files are appended to over time. Read only the slice
your task needs; grep or read further on demand. The biggest
recurring case:

- **`notes/todo.md`** — for "what's the current focus?" read the
  first ~60 lines only (`Read` with `offset=0, limit=60`). That
  covers the intro, `## In Progress`, `## Priorities`, and the
  start of `## Bugs`. Read the full file only when picking up a
  `## Todo` entry, chasing a `[N]` reference, or auditing the
  whole list.

Same principle applies to `done.md` and
`notes/chores/chores-NN.md` — scan headings (`grep '^## '`),
then read the section you need.

**Why:** `notes/todo.md` runs ~370 lines and grows; the routine
acquaint read needs only ~60 of those. Reading the whole file
every session-startup wastes tokens with no information gain.

content.text:
Tooling is file-agnostic — `fix-todo` / `validate-todo` scan whatever file you point them at for `## Todo` / `## Bugs` headers. So pointing them at `notes/bugs.md` just finds `## Bugs` and reports `0 Todo, 1 Bugs`. No code change needed.

**Plan in three pieces, in order:**

1. **Trim `## Todo` intro.** Remove the "Entries carry explicit..." paragraph; add `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` mention into paragraph 1. Drop the "ordered highest-priority first" phrase too (it's now misleading — `## Priorities` is where priority lives).

2. **Move `## Bugs` to `notes/bugs.md`.** Keep `## Bugs` as an H2 header so existing tooling works unchanged. Topped with an `# Bugs` H1 + a one-line intro matching todo.md's shape. Replace the section in todo.md with a one-line pointer `_Bugs live in [bugs.md](bugs.md)._` so discoverability survives.

3. **Update CLAUDE.md** with the file-reads section, calibrated to the slimmer todo.md (limit=~40 lines now covers intro + In Progress + Priorities).

**Small decisions to confirm:**

- **bugs.md header shape:** `# Bugs` H1 file title + `## Bugs` H2 (tool-compat) — slightly redundant, but no tool change. OK?
- **Pointer in todo.md:** keep `## Bugs` heading with a one-line pointer underneath, or drop the heading entirely? I'd keep it — preserves the visual scan.
- **Cycle:** this is enough work (3 commits worth) to be its own cycle. Looks like `0.58.0`, title roughly `refactor: bugs.md split + todo intro trim`. Or amend into the existing uncommitted WC? Per the protocol, cycle is cleaner — opens `-0` Preparation that backfills 0.57.0's `Commits:` ref, bumps Cargo.toml, opens a chores section.

Sound right? Once you confirm, I'd start with Preparation (`0.58.0-0`).

content.content:
990	dogfooded for the first time this cycle. The merge commit
991	at bare `0.56.0` brings 4 sub-step commits (`-0`/`-1`/`-2`/
992	`-3`) back to `main`; `git log --first-parent main` shows
993	one jump from `0.55.0` → `0.56.0`, the sub-steps stay
994	reachable via the merge's second parent.
995	
996	## docs: add --merge todo entry (0.57.0)
997	
998	Adds a `## Todo` entry recording a future `vc-x1 push:
999	--merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to the planned
1000	`--squash`) that automates setting up the non-FF merge
1001	("trapezoid") shape so users don't have to pre-rebase
1002	before `vc-x1 push`. The shape was dogfooded manually in
1003	`0.56.0`; this cycle captures the design as a todo and
1004	walks the Preparation → Work-N → Close-out protocol on a
1005	deliberately small change. The cycle's deliverable was
1006	intended to be one new todo entry, but grew a Work-2 when
1007	-0/-1 surfaced a protocol gap mid-cycle.
1008	
1009	### As-built ladder
1010	
1011	- 0.57.0-0 Preparation:
1012	  - backfill `0.56.0` `Commits:` ref
1013	  - open chores section
1014	  - set up `## In Progress`
1015	- 0.57.0-1 Work-1: add the `--merge` `## Todo` entry #1
1016	  with intro + Preparation/Work-N/Close-out ladder.
1017	- 0.57.0-2 Work-2: CLAUDE.md protocol cleanup added
1018	  mid-cycle after -0/-1 mishandled the close-out → squash
1019	  transition.
1020	  - rename gates to Work review / Commit Description
1021	    review (drop "Gate N" prefixes)
1022	  - add Post close-out review (shape + push decision)
1023	  - add shape-neutral close-out body guidance
1024	  - add semicolons-in-bullets prose rule
1025	  - add Post-amend `jj new` rule
1026	- 0.57.0 Close-out:
1027	  - clear `## In Progress`
1028	  - add `## Done` one-liner
1029	  - finalize chores section
1030	
1031	### Outcome
1032	
1033	Cycle grew from a planned 3 commits to 4 when -0/-1
1034	exposed protocol gaps: no stop-and-ask gate between
1035	close-out commit and any squash/merge/keep action, the
1036	close-out body language assumed the squash shape, and no
1037	guidance about `jj new` after a shape-changing
1038	`jj rebase -r` (surfaced when the merge non-FF setup
1039	left `@` on the merge and git tools read the merge's
1040	content as uncommitted). -2 lands all three fixes — Post
1041	close-out review, shape-neutral close-out body guidance,
1042	and the Post-amend `jj new` rule — plus the gate renames
1043	with descriptive names. The cycle's original deliverable
1044	— the `--merge` Todo entry — was unaffected; the
1045	re-scope landed entirely in CLAUDE.md prose.
1046	
1047	Landed as a non-FF merge — chosen at Post close-out
1048	review. `main` gains one merge commit (`0.57.0`) with
1049	parents `0.56.0` and `-2`; the three sub-step commits
1050	(`-0` / `-1` / `-2`) stay reachable via the merge's
1051	second parent. `git log --first-parent main` reads one
1052	jump from `0.56.0` → `0.57.0`.
1053

file.content:
dogfooded for the first time this cycle. The merge commit
at bare `0.56.0` brings 4 sub-step commits (`-0`/`-1`/`-2`/
`-3`) back to `main`; `git log --first-parent main` shows
one jump from `0.55.0` → `0.56.0`, the sub-steps stay
reachable via the merge's second parent.

## docs: add --merge todo entry (0.57.0)

Adds a `## Todo` entry recording a future `vc-x1 push:
--merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to the planned
`--squash`) that automates setting up the non-FF merge
("trapezoid") shape so users don't have to pre-rebase
before `vc-x1 push`. The shape was dogfooded manually in
`0.56.0`; this cycle captures the design as a todo and
walks the Preparation → Work-N → Close-out protocol on a
deliberately small change. The cycle's deliverable was
intended to be one new todo entry, but grew a Work-2 when
-0/-1 surfaced a protocol gap mid-cycle.

### As-built ladder

- 0.57.0-0 Preparation:
  - backfill `0.56.0` `Commits:` ref
  - open chores section
  - set up `## In Progress`
- 0.57.0-1 Work-1: add the `--merge` `## Todo` entry #1
  with intro + Preparation/Work-N/Close-out ladder.
- 0.57.0-2 Work-2: CLAUDE.md protocol cleanup added
  mid-cycle after -0/-1 mishandled the close-out → squash
  transition.
  - rename gates to Work review / Commit Description
    review (drop "Gate N" prefixes)
  - add Post close-out review (shape + push decision)
  - add shape-neutral close-out body guidance
  - add semicolons-in-bullets prose rule
  - add Post-amend `jj new` rule
- 0.57.0 Close-out:
  - clear `## In Progress`
  - add `## Done` one-liner
  - finalize chores section

### Outcome

Cycle grew from a planned 3 commits to 4 when -0/-1
exposed protocol gaps: no stop-and-ask gate between
close-out commit and any squash/merge/keep action, the
close-out body language assumed the squash shape, and no
guidance about `jj new` after a shape-changing
`jj rebase -r` (surfaced when the merge non-FF setup
left `@` on the merge and git tools read the merge's
content as uncommitted). -2 lands all three fixes — Post
close-out review, shape-neutral close-out body guidance,
and the Post-amend `jj new` rule — plus the gate renames
with descriptive names. The cycle's original deliverable
— the `--merge` Todo entry — was unaffected; the
re-scope landed entirely in CLAUDE.md prose.

Landed as a non-FF merge — chosen at Post close-out
review. `main` gains one merge commit (`0.57.0`) with
parents `0.56.0` and `-2`; the three sub-step commits
(`-0` / `-1` / `-2`) stay reachable via the merge's
second parent. `git log --first-parent main` reads one
jump from `0.56.0` → `0.57.0`.
