1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
7	about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
8	The chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; older ones are closed
9	(chores-09.md and earlier — through the 0.48.x cycles), and the
10	active one ([chores-10.md](chores-10.md), the 0.49.0 cycle onward)
11	also carries the **refactor-tracking** tables (the Context+Params
12	port and the options_flags extraction).
13	
14	App architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
15	generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
16	Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
17	*live status* is in chores-10.md) — lives at the repo root in
18	[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).
19	
20	In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
21	database to better manage the information, TBD.
22	
23	Examples chore file:
24	```
25	# Chores-01.md
26	 
27	General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
28	more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
29	how and why of a particular chore.
30	
31	## Create a binary that lists jj info 
32	
33	This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
34	and using `jj-lib`
35	```
36	
37	## Workflow and conventions
38	
39	Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
40	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:
41	
42	- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
43	  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
44	- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
45	  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
46	  stuck detection.
47	- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
48	  discipline with hard stop after finalize.
49	
50	## Todo format
51	
52	Todo.md is organized into `## In Progress`, `## Todo`, `## Bugs`,
53	and `## Done` sections; each item is a short description with
54	reference links to more detail.
55	
56	`## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` …
57	numbers in the source so you can grep, count, and reference
58	them ("let's work on #3"). You don't hand-maintain the
59	numbers — insert, delete, or reorder entries freely, then
60	`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` renumbers the list and
61	normalizes continuation-line indent. `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone
62	only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo` is the read-only check.
63	The `## Done` section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
64	referenced by number once completed.
65	
66	Reference citations are **double-bracketed** — `[[1]]`, or
67	`[[2]],[[3]]` for several — so the brackets show when rendered
68	(a bare `[1]` renders as just `1`, which doesn't read as a
69	reference). Separate multiple with a comma: `[[2]],[[3]]`, not
70	`[2,3]` (a single ref key — won't resolve) or `[[2]][[3]]`. The
71	`[N]:` definition lines and inline `[text](url)` / `[text](#anchor)`
72	links stay single-bracketed (their brackets already show in
73	source, and the link text reads as a link when rendered).
74	
75	Examples:
76	
77	# Todo
78	- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
79	- Fix bug Y [[1]]
80	
81	# Done
82	- Fixed issue Z [[2]],[[3]]
83	
84	[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
85	[2]: issues.md#issue-z
86	[3]: fixes.md#fix-z
87	
88	## Reference numbering
89	
90	Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
91	file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
92	are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
93	`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
94	different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.
95	
96	Treat `[N]` like a **footnote**: the number is a local slot, only
97	meaningful within its file's `# References`. So a `[N]` *citation*
98	(bare `[N]`, or doubled `[[N]]` — see below) never reuses another
99	file's number; to cite a target a sibling file references, pick
100	your own next-local slot and define it (the same target may carry
101	a different number in each file). A `[N]` *inside a code span*
102	(`` `[72]` ``) is different — that's a quoted identifier: literal
103	text naming a ref-key (often from another file's namespace), data,
104	not a citation; it needs no definition here. To point at a section
105	of another file from prose, use an inline link with an anchor —
106	`[that section](../chores-07.md#…)` — not a bare number.
107	
108	A `chores-NN.md` `# References` entry is usually a
109	`/notes/<file>.md#anchor` (or `/ARCHITECTURE.md`) path, but may
110	also be a **commit reference** —
111	`[N]: <commit-url-with-12-hex-SHA> "<full-40-hex-SHA>"`, cited by
112	a section's `Commits:` line. See
113	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md#chores-commit-references) for the
114	why and the exact shape.
115	
116	A file's `# References` can be **re-packed** to a contiguous
117	`[1]..[N]` in first-citation-appearance order — walk the file's
118	prose in document order (`todo.md`: `## Todo` then `## Done`;
119	`chores-NN.md`: top to bottom) and number refs as their first
120	`[[N]]` citation appears. This is a file-local rewrite — only
121	that file's `[[N]]` citations and `[N]:` definitions move; every
122	target and sibling file is untouched. A `[[N]]` inside a `` ` ``
123	code span is a literal token, not a citation, and is left alone.
124	Do it opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
125	annoy), not on a schedule: `todo.md` fragments fastest (entries
126	land and get pruned every cycle) and is the usual candidate;
127	`chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly and only need it
128	after an unusual event (e.g. a bulk retrofit that allocated slots
129	out of document order).
130	
131	## Retiring Done entries
132	
133	`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
134	work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
135	beats:
136	
137	- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
138	  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
139	  context and migrate them.
140	- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
141	  bumping the version.
142	
143	Migration mechanics:
144	
145	- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
146	  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
147	- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
148	  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
149	  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
150	- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
151	  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
152	  frees the numbers for future reuse.
153

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
The chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; older ones are closed
(chores-09.md and earlier — through the 0.48.x cycles), and the
active one ([chores-10.md](chores-10.md), the 0.49.0 cycle onward)
also carries the **refactor-tracking** tables (the Context+Params
port and the options_flags extraction).

App architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in chores-10.md) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md is organized into `## In Progress`, `## Todo`, `## Bugs`,
and `## Done` sections; each item is a short description with
reference links to more detail.

`## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` …
numbers in the source so you can grep, count, and reference
them ("let's work on #3"). You don't hand-maintain the
numbers — insert, delete, or reorder entries freely, then
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` renumbers the list and
normalizes continuation-line indent. `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone
only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo` is the read-only check.
The `## Done` section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Reference citations are **double-bracketed** — `[[1]]`, or
`[[2]],[[3]]` for several — so the brackets show when rendered
(a bare `[1]` renders as just `1`, which doesn't read as a
reference). Separate multiple with a comma: `[[2]],[[3]]`, not
`[2,3]` (a single ref key — won't resolve) or `[[2]][[3]]`. The
`[N]:` definition lines and inline `[text](url)` / `[text](#anchor)`
links stay single-bracketed (their brackets already show in
source, and the link text reads as a link when rendered).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [[1]]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [[2]],[[3]]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

Treat `[N]` like a **footnote**: the number is a local slot, only
meaningful within its file's `# References`. So a `[N]` *citation*
(bare `[N]`, or doubled `[[N]]` — see below) never reuses another
file's number; to cite a target a sibling file references, pick
your own next-local slot and define it (the same target may carry
a different number in each file). A `[N]` *inside a code span*
(`` `[72]` ``) is different — that's a quoted identifier: literal
text naming a ref-key (often from another file's namespace), data,
not a citation; it needs no definition here. To point at a section
of another file from prose, use an inline link with an anchor —
`[that section](../chores-07.md#…)` — not a bare number.

A `chores-NN.md` `# References` entry is usually a
`/notes/<file>.md#anchor` (or `/ARCHITECTURE.md`) path, but may
also be a **commit reference** —
`[N]: <commit-url-with-12-hex-SHA> "<full-40-hex-SHA>"`, cited by
a section's `Commits:` line. See
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md#chores-commit-references) for the
why and the exact shape.

A file's `# References` can be **re-packed** to a contiguous
`[1]..[N]` in first-citation-appearance order — walk the file's
prose in document order (`todo.md`: `## Todo` then `## Done`;
`chores-NN.md`: top to bottom) and number refs as their first
`[[N]]` citation appears. This is a file-local rewrite — only
that file's `[[N]]` citations and `[N]:` definitions move; every
target and sibling file is untouched. A `[[N]]` inside a `` ` ``
code span is a literal token, not a citation, and is left alone.
Do it opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
annoy), not on a schedule: `todo.md` fragments fastest (entries
land and get pruned every cycle) and is the usual candidate;
`chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly and only need it
after an unusual event (e.g. a bulk retrofit that allocated slots
out of document order).

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":

content.text:
Agreed — your tweak is better, eliminates during-cycle duplication, gives one source of truth.

My read of how it lands:

- **Preparation:** chores section opens with just a `## header` (no body). In Progress in todo.md carries the cycle title + overview + ladder.
- **During cycle:** only In Progress is updated (ladder marks go `(current)` → `(done)`, text revised as the plan shifts). Chores stays a bare header.
- **Close-out:** cut the In Progress body, paste under the chores header. Replace In Progress with `_No cycle currently in progress._`. Add `### As-built ladder` / `### Outcome` if useful.

Two things to confirm:

1. **The header during cycle = anticipated close-out title** (e.g. `## refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)`), not Preparation's own `(0.58.0-0)` title — since the section will eventually hold the whole-cycle content. Right?

2. **One chores section per cycle as default.** Matches recent practice (0.56.0/0.57.0 each have one section). Per-commit sections (like 0.55.0's four) become a deliberate choice, not the default.

CLAUDE.md `### Preparation` + `### Close-out` text need updating to describe the new mechanic — fits this cycle's `-3` or close-out.

Once you confirm (1), I'll trim `chores-12.md` to the title-only form, then ready for your In Progress body tweak.

message.content:
now the chores intro paragraph.

The paragraph is your typical wordy style. Instead it should be
a succinct problem statement followed by bullet-list "plan" 
which provides what we're doing when.

Ideally the chore in Todo is structured that way, but the bullet-list

Thoughts

content.text:
Agreed on the shape — "succinct problem + bullet plan" is sharper than the current `## Prose form` rule ("short intro then bullets"), which gets stretched into paragraphs in practice. Three thoughts:

1. **What the shape captures.** Problem statement = the *why*, one or two sentences. Plan bullets = the *what/when*. Keeping them separate keeps each honest — the problem doesn't get padded with intent, and the plan can shift without rewriting the problem.

2. **In Progress vs. Todo entry differ only in plan formality.**
   - **In Progress** plan: numbered ladder (`-0`, `-1`, …), `(current)`/`(done)` markers — it's a committed sequence.
   - **Todo entry** plan: rough bullets, no numbering, no markers — it's a sketch that gets formalized only when the entry is picked up. Worth saying explicitly in CLAUDE.md so the two don't get conflated.

3. **Retrofitting existing Todo entries is its own work.** Most current entries are paragraph-style. A sweep to convert them to "problem + bullet plan" is a separate effort — add as a new `## Todo` line in this cycle's `-1` commit, not land in `-1` itself.

Proposed rewrite of In Progress (intro shrinks from ~12 lines to 2):

```
**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md
- 0.58.0-3 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`
```

The CLAUDE.md convention update (formalize "problem + bullet plan" + the In-Progress-vs-Todo formality distinction) fits in `-3` or close-out.

Want me to apply the rewrite and proceed?

content.text:
**0.58.0-1 — Commit Description review.**

Title (49 chars):

```
docs: add Priorities + trim Todo intro (0.58.0-1)
```

Body:

```
Adds `## Priorities` between `## In Progress` and `## Todo`
to make priority calls stable and reviewable. Tiered list
(`P1` = highest), referencing `## Todo` entries by
`**prefix...**` (unique starts-with) or free-form lines.
Replaces ad-hoc in-conversation reordering, which is fragile
because Todo entry numbers shift under `vc-x1 fix-todo`.

Trims the `## Todo` intro to drop the numbering-mechanics
paragraph (duplicated in `notes/README.md`) and the now-stale
"ordered highest-priority first" phrase; folds a one-line
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` mention into the survivor.

- notes/todo.md

input.content:
# Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix for.
Each entry describes what goes wrong, when, and the cost of
the failure. Entries are numbered (`1.` `2.` …) the same way
as `## Todo` in `todo.md`; run
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/bugs.md` to renumber after
insert / delete / reorder.

## Bugs

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

# References

message.content: LG, but I want to extract todo-backlog.md either do it now or make it -3 and update CLAUDE.md -4, thoughts

content.text:
Lean toward `-3` separate. Two reasons:

1. **Scope honesty.** `-2` is "split Bugs" — clean, mechanical. Bundling Backlog in mixes two structural changes into one commit; per-commit reverts get harder.
2. **The Backlog split is meatier than Bugs was** — needs design decisions worth surfacing before I dive in.

Complications I'd want to nail down before `-3`:

- **References split.** `todo.md > # References` has `[1]`–`[33]`. Some are cited only by `## Todo` body (move with Backlog), some only by `## Done` (stay), some by both (duplicate? canonical owner?). Needs a sweep, not a rename.
- **Priorities convention update.** "A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title" — after split, that's a cross-file resolution against `todo-backlog.md`. One-line wording change, but worth being explicit.
- **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` invocations.** Today users run them on `todo.md`. Now `## Todo` numbering lives in `todo-backlog.md` — needs a one-liner in `notes/README.md`. (Tooling itself works unchanged — file-agnostic.)
- **`## Todo` heading in todo.md.** Keep as pointer to `todo-backlog.md` (parallel to `## Bugs`)? Or drop entirely? I'd keep — preserves visual scan symmetry.

Cycle becomes 5 commits + close-out: `-0` Prep, `-1` Priorities+intro, `-2` Bugs, `-3` Backlog, `-4` CLAUDE.md expansion, `0.58.0` close-out.

Suggest: ship `-2` now as drafted, then before `-3` I do the ref-citation sweep and we agree on Backlog mechanics. Sound right?

message.content: ship -2, and then let's just talk about todo-backlog.md

content.text:
Shipped. On `todo-backlog.md`:

**Filename.** `todo-backlog.md` reads cleanest — descriptive, no collision with existing files, "backlog" is universally understood as the long-tail list. Alternative `backlog.md` is shorter but loses the `todo-` prefix grouping. I'd go `todo-backlog.md`.

**The interesting design point — references.** todo.md's `# References` is `[1]`–`[33]`. Some refs are cited only by `## Todo` body, some only by `## Done`, some by both. Three approaches:

1. **Per-file re-pack.** Each file (todo.md, todo-backlog.md) gets its own freshly-numbered `# References` section containing only the refs cited by *its* body. Refs cited by both get duplicated (different numbers in each file is fine — per the existing "Reference numbering" convention, numbers are file-local). Cleanest end state; one-shot ref re-pack per file per the README's documented mechanic.

2. **Move-only.** Move every ref to whichever file cites it; for refs cited by both, pick canonical owner and have the other file cite via cross-file link. More mechanical, less duplication, but introduces cross-file ref chains.

3. **Leave canonical in todo.md, point from todo-backlog.md.** Easiest commit but creates a permanent cross-file dependency for ref resolution.

I'd recommend **(1)** — it matches the existing "re-pack opportunistically" convention and leaves each file self-contained. The duplication cost is low (refs are one-liners) and self-containment makes future moves easy.

**Other small decisions:**

- **`## Todo` heading in todo.md** → keep as `_See [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md)._` (parallel to Bugs).
- **Priorities convention** → one-line update: "A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title **in `todo-backlog.md`**".
- **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` invocation docs** → one-liner in notes/README.md noting backlog is the file to pass.

**Out-of-scope question worth flagging:** `## Done` in todo.md still holds 0.51.0-0.57.0 entries (~7 lines). Worth extracting too, or leave for a future cycle? I'd leave it — it's small, recent context is genuinely useful at todo.md head, and there's no equivalent "too big" pressure.

Thoughts on the references approach?

message.content:
Yes 1.

Are you suggesting todo.md only has ## In Progress and ## Priorities and everything else is in todo-backlog?

content.text:
No — my proposal keeps `## Done` in todo.md. The shape I had in mind:

- **todo.md** (live focus): intro, `## In Progress`, `## Priorities`, `## Todo` (pointer), `## Bugs` (pointer), `## Done` (rolling buffer of recent ships), `# References` (Done-only refs).
- **todo-backlog.md**: intro, `## Todo` (entries), `# References` (Todo-only refs).
- **bugs.md**: already done.
- **done.md**: unchanged; gets entries migrated from `## Done` per the existing rolling-buffer rule.

**Why I'd keep `## Done` in todo.md** — it's *by design* a rolling buffer per `notes/README.md > ## Retiring Done entries`. Recent ship context is genuinely useful next to In Progress/Priorities ("what just landed?" frames "what next?"). Moving it out would erase the rolling-buffer concept and make done.md churn every close-out.

**But it's a real call** — if you want todo.md as a strict "currently-active" surface (In Progress + Priorities + pointers, nothing else), I'd push back, not refuse. The aggressive split saves ~10-15 lines and gives perfect symmetry (every historical/cold surface pointed to a sibling file). Trade-off is losing the at-a-glance recent-ship signal.

Your call on Done. Keep it, or extract it too?

message.content:
Keeping ## Done in ## Todo feels right. What about the items in ## Priorities?
As an experiment I just made it a bulleted list and added ## The list because
the intro paragraph is bulleted so it was hard to differentiate.

But if we leave the entries in "The List" they need to turn into references.
I'm leaning towards entries in "The List" don't move to todo-backlog but stay
in todo and we use the simple prefix and then for those they are local, and
it means you don't need to waste time and energy reading todo-backlog.

Thoughts?

content.text:
Your instinct is right — cross-file traversal for routine priority work defeats the goal. Keep prefix-match local.

**Yes to:** Entries that Priorities cites stay in todo.md; non-cited entries live in todo-backlog.md. Prefix-match resolves within todo.md. Bot read of todo.md head sees Priorities + the prioritized entries together.

**Bullet collision is real** — convention intro bullets and tier entries look the same. Two fixes:

1. **Sub-heading approach (your experiment).** `## Priorities` holds the convention; a second heading (`## The list`, `## Active`, `## Now`, etc.) holds the P-lines. Works but adds a heading.
2. **Tier sub-headings.** Keep one `## Priorities` section; group entries under `### P1` / `### P2` / `### P3` sub-headings. Convention bullets stay at top; entries are visually separated by the `###` headings, no extra `##` needed.

I'd lean (2) — one fewer heading, and the P-tier semantics surface in the structure rather than being implicit in line prefixes.

**Naming the prioritized-entries section in todo.md** (whether it's `## The list` or just stays `## Todo`):

- **Keep `## Todo`** — same heading in both files; `vc-x1 fix-todo`/`validate-todo` just work on each. Conversational refs ("work on #3") need file context, but priority context usually implies it.
- **New name** (`## Active`, `## Now`) — semantically clearer but introduces a heading that tools don't recognize. Would need a tooling update or a one-off invocation rule.

I'd lean keep `## Todo`. Same name in two files is fine since file-local tools work file-locally; the small "which file?" friction is acceptable.

**One trade-off to acknowledge:** promotion/demotion = file move (cut from todo.md `## Todo`, paste to todo-backlog.md `## Todo`, run `fix-todo` on both). Small operational cost; OK if priorities shift slowly.

Want to go with: prefix-match stays local; prioritized entries stay in todo.md `## Todo`; non-prioritized to todo-backlog.md `## Todo`; convention vs entries visually separated by tier sub-headings inside `## Priorities`?

284	44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
285	    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
286	    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
287	    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
288	    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
289	    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
290	    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
291	    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
292	    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
293	    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
294	    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
295	    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
296	    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
297	    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
298	    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
299	    it adds value over the resolution chain.
300	45. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
301	    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
302	    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
303	    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
304	    lines / typos surface.
305	
306	## Bugs
307	
308	_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._
309	
310	## Done
311	
312	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
313	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
314	
315	_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._
316	
317	- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
318	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
319	- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
320	- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
321	- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
322	- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]
323	- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[33]]
324

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

P1: chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md
P2: `**single-field...**`
P3: `**por -> dual...**`
P3: `**por/duality parity...**`

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future. Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines. Detailed motivation, safety
 requirements, and ordering belong in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via
 `[N]` ref. Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` to renumber
 after insert / delete / reorder.

1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
     sub-step in the merge.
   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[4]]
8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
20. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
21. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
22. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
23. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
24. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
25. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
26. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
27. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
28. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
29. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
30. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
31. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[20]]
32. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
33. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
34. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
35. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
36. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
37. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
38. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
39. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
40. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
41. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
42. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
43. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
44. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.
45. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
    lines / typos surface.

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[33]]

content.text:
Pausing on a couple of small decisions before the big mechanical moves:

**1. Typo in Priorities.** `P3: **por/duality parity...**` is meant to match Todo #7 `**por/dual parity + dual → por conversion**` — but the prefix has "por/duality" vs the entry's "por/dual". Either fix Priorities to `**por/dual parity...**` (preferred — entry text is canonical), or rename Todo #7. I'll fix Priorities unless you say otherwise.

**2. Stayers (prioritized entries that remain in todo.md `## Todo`):**
- Todo #2 → new #1: `**single-field options_flags leaves → value field**`
- Todo #5 → new #2: `**por → dual conversion**`
- Todo #7 → new #3: `**por/dual parity + dual → por conversion**`

The other 42 entries move to todo-backlog.md.

**3. Pointer wording in todo.md `## Todo` intro.** Proposed:

```
## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.
```

**4. todo-backlog.md intro** — sibling shape to todo.md `## Todo`:

```
# Todo Backlog

 Non-prioritized `## Todo` entries — the long tail. When an
 entry becomes a priority, move it (and any refs it cites)
 to `notes/todo.md > ## Todo` and add a tier line under the
 right `### Pn` in `notes/todo.md > ## Priorities`.

 Same formatting rules as `notes/todo.md > ## Todo` — see
 `notes/README.md > ## Todo format`. Run
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo-backlog.md` to
 renumber.

## Todo

[42 entries]

# References

[26 entries]
```

**5. Re-pack plan** — both files re-pack `# References` from `[1]` in first-citation order. todo.md ends with 8 refs (1 for the staying Todo entries, 7 for Done). todo-backlog.md ends with 26 refs (all the ones the moving Todo entries cite). Shared refs (e.g. the old `[32]` cited by both Todo #1 and Done) duplicate — each file gets its own slot with the same URL.

OK with 1-5? Then I'll execute the moves.

input.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (current)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

# References

[1]: /notes/por-dual-parity.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-move-chores-under-noteschores-0510
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-close-sb_ide-elimination-0520
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-todo-renumber--fix-todopy-0530
[5]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#refactor-scope-cli-cleanup-0540
[6]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#feat-validate-todo--fix-todo-0550
[7]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-refine-cycle-protocol-0560
[8]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-add---merge-todo-entry-0570

input.content:
# Todo Backlog

 Non-prioritized `## Todo` entries — the long tail. When an
 entry becomes a priority, move it (and any refs it cites)
 to `notes/todo.md > ## Todo` and add a tier line under the
 right `### Pn` in `notes/todo.md > ## Priorities`.

 Same formatting rules as `notes/todo.md > ## Todo` — see
 `notes/README.md > ## Todo format`. Run
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo-backlog.md` to
 renumber.

## Todo

1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#23);
   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[1]]
   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
     sub-step in the merge.
   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
2. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
3. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
4. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
5. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[2]],[[3]]
6. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[4]]
7. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[5]]
8. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
   with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
   (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
   `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
   close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
   (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
   deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[6]]
9. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
   `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
   the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
   across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
   coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
10. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
11. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[7]]
12. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[8]]
13. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[9]],[[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
14. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
15. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
16. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[13]]
17. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[13]]
18. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
19. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
20. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
21. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[14]].
22. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
23. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[9]]
24. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[15]]
25. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
26. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[16]]
27. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[14]]
28. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[17]]
29. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[9]]
30. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
31. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[18]]
32. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[19]]
33. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[20]]
34. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[21]]
35. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
36. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
37. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
38. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[22]],[[23]]
39. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[24]]
40. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[25]],[[26]]
41. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.
42. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
    lines / typos surface.

   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[4]]
7. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[5]]
8. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
   with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
   (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
   `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
   close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
   (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
   deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[6]]
9. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
   `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
   the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
   across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
   coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
10. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
11. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[7]]
12. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[8]]
13. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[9]],[[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
14. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
15. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[10]],[[11]],[[12]]
16. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[13]]
17. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[13]]
18. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
19. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
20. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
21. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[14]].
22. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
23. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[9]]
24. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[15]]
25. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
26. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[16]]
27. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[14]]
28. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[17]]
29. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[9]]
30. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
31. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[18]]
32. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[19]]
33. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[20]]
34. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[21]]
35. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
36. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
37. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
38. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[22]],[[23]]
39. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[24]]
40. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[25]],[[26]]
41. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.
42. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
    lines / typos surface.
