toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (current)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (done)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

# References

[1]: /notes/por-dual-parity.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-move-chores-under-noteschores-0510
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-close-sb_ide-elimination-0520
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-todo-renumber--fix-todopy-0530
[5]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#refactor-scope-cli-cleanup-0540
[6]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#feat-validate-todo--fix-todo-0550
[7]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-refine-cycle-protocol-0560
[8]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-add---merge-todo-entry-0570

file.content:
## File reads — read the slice you need

`notes/todo.md` is read on most session-startups for current
focus. Read only the first ~60 lines by default — `Read` with
`offset=0, limit=60`. That covers the intro, `## In Progress`,
and `## Priorities`. Read the full file only when picking up a
`## Todo` entry, chasing a `[N]` reference, or auditing the
whole list.

**Why:** the file runs ~370 lines and grows; the routine
acquaint read needs only ~60. Reading the whole file every
session wastes tokens with no information gain.

## Memory

Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
CLAUDE.md it provides no capability CLAUDE.md doesn't already cover,
and it loses on discoverability:

- **CLAUDE.md** — at the repo root, a well-known location for bot

input.new_string:
## File reads — read the slice you need

Long notes files are appended to over time. Read only the
slice your task needs; grep or read further on demand.

- **`notes/todo.md`** (the routine acquaint read) — first
  ~60 lines covers intro + `## In Progress` + `## Priorities`.
  `Read` with `offset=0, limit=60`. Read further only when
  picking up a `## Todo` entry, chasing a `[N]` ref, or
  auditing the whole list.
- **`notes/todo-backlog.md`** — the long-tail backlog
  (non-prioritized `## Todo` entries). Read only when
  picking up a backlog item; grep to locate it first.
- **`notes/bugs.md`** — the bug list. Small; read whole
  when triaging a bug or chasing the `## Bugs` pointer in
  todo.md.
- **`notes/done.md`** + **`notes/chores/chores-NN.md`** —
  historical / append-mostly. Scan headings first
  (`grep '^## ' notes/chores/chores-NN.md`), then read only
  the section you need.

**Why:** before the 0.58.0 split, `notes/todo.md` ran ~370
lines and grew every cycle. The split moved the backlog and
bugs into sibling files so the routine read stays small; the
same "slice you need" rule applies to historical files.

content.text:
0.58.0-4 shipped. Before close-out — a scope call.

The cycle also produced two CLAUDE.md protocol changes worth codifying (we agreed on them in conversation but they aren't in CLAUDE.md yet):

1. **Chores section title-only during cycle, move In Progress block into it at close-out.** Affects `### Preparation` ("Header = provisional commit title; intro that mirrors...") and `### Close-out`.
2. **Problem statement + bullet plan as the shape** for In Progress and chores intros (and informally for Todo entries). Touches `## Prose form`.

Two ways to land these:

- **Option A — one close-out (0.58.0):** Cargo bump + In Progress clear + Done entry + chores-12 finalize + notes/README.md updates + CLAUDE.md protocol codification, all in one commit. Bigger close-out than usual.
- **Option B — add `0.58.0-5` (CLAUDE.md protocol codification), then 0.58.0 close-out (just bookkeeping):** Cleaner per-commit story; keeps close-out true to "bookkeeping only" per the protocol body-shape rule.

I'd lean **B** — the CLAUDE.md protocol changes are substantive content, not bookkeeping. Close-out then stays small and clean.

Either way, the final close-out title needs syncing — current chores-12 header says `refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)` but the cycle did more (Priorities, todo-backlog split, File reads rule). Proposed: `refactor: notes/todo restructure (0.58.0)` (42 chars; umbrella term that covers everything).

A or B? And OK on the final title?

lastPrompt: go

message.content: B

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (done)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores (done)
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

[4]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-todo-renumber--fix-todopy-0530
[5]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#refactor-scope-cli-cleanup-0540
[6]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#feat-validate-todo--fix-todo-0550
[7]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-refine-cycle-protocol-0560
[8]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-add---merge-todo-entry-0570

content.content:
107	- **Post-amend `jj new`** — `jj edit <rev>`,
108	  `jj rebase -r <rev> -d ...`, and `jj squash --into <rev>`
109	  all leave `@` on the rewritten commit rather than on an
110	  empty change above. The most common case is the close-out
111	  push for a [Merge (non-FF)](#pushing) shape (`jj rebase
112	  -r <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>` leaves `@` on the new
113	  merge). In a colocated jj+git repo, git then detaches
114	  HEAD at the commit's first parent, so `git status`,
115	  gitk, and IDE diff views read the commit's content as
116	  uncommitted relative to HEAD. Follow up with `jj new` to
117	  create an empty `@` above; HEAD shifts to the commit and
118	  the working tree reads clean.
119	
120	## Prose form
121	
122	Long-lived prose on this project follows one shape: a short intro
123	that explains the *why* or the high-level *what*, then a `-` bullet
124	list for the details. Wrap lines at ≤72 cols (bullet continuations
125	indent two spaces). Avoid wall-of-prose paragraphs — they hide the
126	structure that bullets make scannable.
127	
128	Surfaces that use this shape:
129	
130	- Module / function / struct / field doc comments in `.rs` files —
131	  see [Doc comments](#doc-comments-on-every-file-function-and-method).
132	- Commit message bodies (both app-repo and session-repo). The
133	  ≤50-col title is the commit-specific add-on; see
134	  [Per-commit flow](#per-commit-flow).
135	- Chore descriptions in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` — see
136	  [Chores section content](#chores-section-content--no-edit-list-git-is-the-record).
137	- Todo and Done entries in `notes/todo.md` when an entry needs more
138	  than one line of detail. Pure one-liners are still fine.
139	
140	Bullet *content* differs by surface:
141	
142	- **Commit bodies** — bullets are file-by-file: one bullet per file
143	  changed, file plus a one-line gist (e.g.
144	  `README.md: new Overview intro`). Source of truth for the
145	  mechanical edit list.
146	- **Chores / todo / done** — bullets are conceptual (design points,
147	  structural notes, the "what landed and why" at a notch above
148	  file-list granularity). Never a copy of the commit's edit list —
149	  see [Chores section content](#chores-section-content--no-edit-list-git-is-the-record).
150	- **Doc comments** — bullets are whatever structure fits (fields,
151	  cases, invariants).
152	
153	**Semicolons inside bullets.** A bullet that joins
154	multiple clauses with semicolons (`A; B; C`) is a list
155	hiding inside running prose — break the clauses into
156	sub-bullets so the structure shows. Semicolons in running

## Prose form

Long-lived prose on this project follows one shape: a short intro
that explains the *why* or the high-level *what*, then a `-` bullet
list for the details. Wrap lines at ≤72 cols (bullet continuations
indent two spaces). Avoid wall-of-prose paragraphs — they hide the
structure that bullets make scannable.

Surfaces that use this shape:

- Module / function / struct / field doc comments in `.rs` files —

- Commit message bodies (both app-repo and session-repo). The
  ≤50-col title is the commit-specific add-on; see

- Chore descriptions in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` — see

- Todo and Done entries in `notes/todo.md` when an entry needs more

Bullet *content* differs by surface:

- **Commit bodies** — bullets are file-by-file: one bullet per file

  `README.md: new Overview intro`). Source of truth for the
  mechanical edit list.
- **Chores / todo / done** — bullets are conceptual (design points,
  structural notes, the "what landed and why" at a notch above
  file-list granularity). Never a copy of the commit's edit list —

- **Doc comments** — bullets are whatever structure fits (fields,
  cases, invariants).

**Semicolons inside bullets.** A bullet that joins
multiple clauses with semicolons (`A; B; C`) is a list

content.content:
290	Close-out. One protocol; push and squash are actions you take
291	when wanted, not modes chosen up front. The mechanics below
292	run in the order they happen.
293	
294	### Numbering
295	
296	The version suffix on each commit carries its phase, with one
297	separator — `.` — at every depth (semver mandates `-` once at
298	the start of the prerelease, then `.` for each level after):
299	
300	- `X.Y.Z-0` — Preparation
301	- `X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, … — Work commits
302	- `X.Y.Z` — Close-out (bare version, no suffix)
303	
304	A **trailing `0` identifier marks a Preparation**, and the
305	rule nests to any depth: when a Work commit needs subdividing
306	into its own sub-cycle, append another level — `X.Y.Z-3.0`
307	(Preparation of the `-3` sub-cycle), `X.Y.Z-3.1` /
308	`X.Y.Z-3.2` (its Work), `X.Y.Z-3` (its Close-out),
309	`X.Y.Z-3.1.0` (Preparation of the `-3.1` sub-sub-cycle), and
310	so on. A sub-cycle needing no Preparation just omits the `.0`
311	(`-3.1`, `-3.2`, `-3`); one that grows a Preparation later
312	adds `-3.0` with no renumbering of siblings.
313	
314	Each commit carries its phase version in its title **and**
315	`Cargo.toml` — bump `Cargo.toml` at the start of each phase,
316	so `vc-x1 -V` shows the active phase at build time.
317	
318	**Ordering caveat.** The one ordering guarantee semver gives
319	is that the bare `X.Y.Z` close-out outranks every `X.Y.Z-…`
320	dev version. Within the ladder it does *not* hold for nested
321	close-outs — `X.Y.Z-3` sorts *before* its children
322	`X.Y.Z-3.0` / `X.Y.Z-3.1`, because a prefix always precedes
323	its extensions. Cosmetic — nothing depends on within-ladder
324	order — but don't claim monotonicity.
325	
326	**Why numeric suffixes** (`-0`, `-1`, …) rather than `-devN`:
327	semver pre-release identifiers compare per spec and Cargo
328	accepts them. `-dev` adds no information the git log doesn't
329	carry and doubles typing per commit. The final close-out
330	commit (bare `X.Y.Z`) signals completion rather than amending
331	prior commits — history stays readable, exploratory vs final
332	commits obvious.
333	
334	### Preparation
335	
336	The cycle's first commit (`X.Y.Z-0`):
337	
338	- **Backfill the previous cycle's chores `Commits:` ref.**
339	  Grab the just-pushed commit's URL + full SHA, fill in the
340	  newest chores section that was left `Commits:`-less. See
341	  [Chores commit references](#chores-commit-references).
342	- **Bump the version** in `Cargo.toml` to `X.Y.Z-0`.
343	- **Pick up a `## Todo` item** (if the cycle has one). Move
344	  that entry's text out of `## Todo` and into `## In
345	  Progress` — its overview and to-do list, followed by the
346	  plan ladder — so `## In Progress` is self-describing, not
347	  a bare ladder. A cycle that merges several entangled
348	  `## Todo` items moves all of them. A `## Todo` entry that
349	  duplicates current `## In Progress` work is a process bug.
350	- **Open the chores section.** Header = provisional commit
351	  title; intro that **mirrors** the `## In Progress` content
352	  so the cycle stays readable once `## In Progress` is
353	  cleared at close-out; any `###` design subsections. No
354	  per-file edit list — that lives in the commit body.
355	
356	### Per-commit flow
357	
358	Every commit (Preparation, each Work commit, Close-out) goes
359	through:
360	
361	1. **Mark this commit `(current)`** as the first edit in
362	   `notes/todo.md > ## In Progress`, before any code/doc
363	   work.
364	2. **Do the work.**
365	3. **Flip this commit `(current)` → `(done)`** in `## In
366	   Progress` — before the cargo cycle and the commit, so
367	   the commit captures the completed state.
368	4. **Cargo cycle** (skip-able for purely-docs commits; full
369	   cycle mandatory at close-out):
370	   1. `cargo fmt`
371	   2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
372	   3. `cargo test`
373	   4. `cargo install --path . --locked`
374	   5. (re-test if anything substantive changed)
375	5. **Work review.** Stop *before* writing any description;
376	   tell the user "ready to commit." The user reviews the
377	   working-copy changes in their editor. Most change
378	   requests come here — iterate until the work is right.
379	6. **Write the commit description.** [Conventional

file.content:
Close-out. One protocol; push and squash are actions you take
when wanted, not modes chosen up front. The mechanics below
run in the order they happen.

### Numbering

The version suffix on each commit carries its phase, with one
separator — `.` — at every depth (semver mandates `-` once at
the start of the prerelease, then `.` for each level after):

- `X.Y.Z-0` — Preparation
- `X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, … — Work commits
- `X.Y.Z` — Close-out (bare version, no suffix)

A **trailing `0` identifier marks a Preparation**, and the
rule nests to any depth: when a Work commit needs subdividing
into its own sub-cycle, append another level — `X.Y.Z-3.0`
(Preparation of the `-3` sub-cycle), `X.Y.Z-3.1` /
`X.Y.Z-3.2` (its Work), `X.Y.Z-3` (its Close-out),
`X.Y.Z-3.1.0` (Preparation of the `-3.1` sub-sub-cycle), and
so on. A sub-cycle needing no Preparation just omits the `.0`
(`-3.1`, `-3.2`, `-3`); one that grows a Preparation later
adds `-3.0` with no renumbering of siblings.

Each commit carries its phase version in its title **and**
`Cargo.toml` — bump `Cargo.toml` at the start of each phase,
so `vc-x1 -V` shows the active phase at build time.

**Ordering caveat.** The one ordering guarantee semver gives
is that the bare `X.Y.Z` close-out outranks every `X.Y.Z-…`
dev version. Within the ladder it does *not* hold for nested
close-outs — `X.Y.Z-3` sorts *before* its children
`X.Y.Z-3.0` / `X.Y.Z-3.1`, because a prefix always precedes
its extensions. Cosmetic — nothing depends on within-ladder
order — but don't claim monotonicity.

**Why numeric suffixes** (`-0`, `-1`, …) rather than `-devN`:
semver pre-release identifiers compare per spec and Cargo
accepts them. `-dev` adds no information the git log doesn't
carry and doubles typing per commit. The final close-out
commit (bare `X.Y.Z`) signals completion rather than amending
prior commits — history stays readable, exploratory vs final
commits obvious.

### Preparation

The cycle's first commit (`X.Y.Z-0`):

- **Backfill the previous cycle's chores `Commits:` ref.**
  Grab the just-pushed commit's URL + full SHA, fill in the
  newest chores section that was left `Commits:`-less. See
  [Chores commit references](#chores-commit-references).
- **Bump the version** in `Cargo.toml` to `X.Y.Z-0`.
- **Pick up a `## Todo` item** (if the cycle has one). Move
  that entry's text out of `## Todo` and into `## In
  Progress` — its overview and to-do list, followed by the
  plan ladder — so `## In Progress` is self-describing, not
  a bare ladder. A cycle that merges several entangled
  `## Todo` items moves all of them. A `## Todo` entry that
  duplicates current `## In Progress` work is a process bug.
- **Open the chores section.** Header = provisional commit
  title; intro that **mirrors** the `## In Progress` content
  so the cycle stays readable once `## In Progress` is
  cleared at close-out; any `###` design subsections. No
  per-file edit list — that lives in the commit body.

### Per-commit flow

Every commit (Preparation, each Work commit, Close-out) goes
through:

1. **Mark this commit `(current)`** as the first edit in
   `notes/todo.md > ## In Progress`, before any code/doc
   work.
2. **Do the work.**
3. **Flip this commit `(current)` → `(done)`** in `## In
   Progress` — before the cargo cycle and the commit, so
   the commit captures the completed state.
4. **Cargo cycle** (skip-able for purely-docs commits; full
   cycle mandatory at close-out):
   1. `cargo fmt`
   2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
   3. `cargo test`
   4. `cargo install --path . --locked`
   5. (re-test if anything substantive changed)
5. **Work review.** Stop *before* writing any description;
   tell the user "ready to commit." The user reviews the
   working-copy changes in their editor. Most change
   requests come here — iterate until the work is right.
6. **Write the commit description.** [Conventional

content.content:
474	### Close-out
475	
476	The cycle's last commit (bare `X.Y.Z`):
477	
478	- **Move the picked-up item** from `## In Progress` to a
479	  one-line entry (with a chores `[N]` ref) in `## Done`.
480	- **Finalize the chores section.** Sync the section header
481	  to the **final** commit title and update every markdown
482	  anchor back-reference to it; add an `### As-built ladder`
483	  listing the cycle's commits; `### Outcome` notes if
484	  useful.
485	- **Update `notes/README.md`** if functionality changed
486	  (new flags, new subcommands, changed behavior).
487	
488	The close-out commit's body should describe **only what's
489	in that commit** (the bookkeeping: clearing `## In
490	Progress`, the `## Done` entry, chores section finalize)
491	— not what would happen post-squash. Squash / merge /
492	keep is a separate push-time decision (Post close-out
493	review, see [Pushing](#pushing) below); keeping the
494	close-out body shape-neutral avoids locking in
495	squash-language before the decision is made.
496	
497	Whether to **squash** the cycle into one commit before the
498	publishing push, or push as-is, is decided at push time —
499	see [Pushing](#pushing).
500	
501	### Pushing
502	
503	**Push is discretionary** — done when you want to back up
504	work or publish interim progress, and **always at
505	close-out** (the cycle's result must be published). Interim
506	pushes are a judgment call; nothing forces push-per-commit.
507	
508	**Post close-out review (close-out push only).** After

file.content:
### Close-out

The cycle's last commit (bare `X.Y.Z`):

- **Move the picked-up item** from `## In Progress` to a
  one-line entry (with a chores `[N]` ref) in `## Done`.
- **Finalize the chores section.** Sync the section header
  to the **final** commit title and update every markdown
  anchor back-reference to it; add an `### As-built ladder`
  listing the cycle's commits; `### Outcome` notes if
  useful.
- **Update `notes/README.md`** if functionality changed
  (new flags, new subcommands, changed behavior).

The close-out commit's body should describe **only what's
in that commit** (the bookkeeping: clearing `## In
Progress`, the `## Done` entry, chores section finalize)
— not what would happen post-squash. Squash / merge /
keep is a separate push-time decision (Post close-out
review, see [Pushing](#pushing) below); keeping the
close-out body shape-neutral avoids locking in
squash-language before the decision is made.

Whether to **squash** the cycle into one commit before the
publishing push, or push as-is, is decided at push time —
see [Pushing](#pushing).

### Pushing

**Push is discretionary** — done when you want to back up
work or publish interim progress, and **always at
close-out** (the cycle's result must be published). Interim
pushes are a judgment call; nothing forces push-per-commit.

**Post close-out review (close-out push only).** After

input.new_string:
- **Pick up a `## Todo` item** (if the cycle has one). Move
  that entry's text out of `## Todo` and into `## In
  Progress` — a **bold title line** (exact match for the
  chores section header, minus the `## ` prefix) +
  **succinct problem statement** + **plan ladder**. See
  [Prose form](#prose-form) (Problem + plan shape). A cycle
  that merges several entangled `## Todo` items moves all
  of them. A `## Todo` entry that duplicates current
  `## In Progress` work is a process bug.
- **Open the chores section.** Header = the cycle's
  **anticipated close-out title** (e.g. `## refactor: foo

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (done)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores (done)
- 0.58.0-5 codify in CLAUDE.md: chores title-only during cycle,
  In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan
  shape for In Progress / chores intros / Todo entries (current)
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

# References

[1]: /notes/por-dual-parity.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-move-chores-under-noteschores-0510
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-close-sb_ide-elimination-0520
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-todo-renumber--fix-todopy-0530
[5]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#refactor-scope-cli-cleanup-0540
[6]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#feat-validate-todo--fix-todo-0550
[7]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-refine-cycle-protocol-0560
[8]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#docs-add---merge-todo-entry-0570

content.text:
**0.58.0-5 — Commit Description review.**

Title (50 chars):

```
docs: codify cycle protocol updates (0.58.0-5)
```

Body:

```
Three conventions iterated during this cycle land in CLAUDE.md
so future cycles follow the same shape from Preparation onward.

- CLAUDE.md
  -- `### Preparation`: chores section opens with the
     anticipated close-out title as the `##` header, no body;
     `## In Progress` carries the live narrative; one chores
     section per cycle is the default
  -- `### Preparation`: `## Todo` pickup spec sharpened —
     bold title line (exact match for chores header) +
     succinct problem statement + plan ladder
  -- `### Close-out`: new "move `## In Progress` block into
     chores section" step — cut the body, drop the bold
     title line (chores `##` carries it), paste under the
     bare header
  -- `## Prose form`: new "Problem + plan shape" callout —
     `## In Progress`, chores intros, and `## Todo` entries
     use a succinct-problem + plan-bullets shape; ladder
     formality differs (In Progress / chores numbered,
     Todo entries informal)
- notes/todo.md `## In Progress`: mark 0.58.0-5 (done)

ochid: /.claude/pxqmtusnorrp
```

One design call I made without explicit confirmation: at close-out, the bold title line of the In Progress block gets **dropped** when moving into chores (since the chores `##` header carries the same string). The alternative is keeping it as the section's first line (redundant but mirrors the in-progress look). I went with drop — flag if you'd rather keep it.

OK to commit?

input.content:
# Chores-12

Continuation of `chores-11.md` (which is closed at `0.57.0` —
the `--merge` todo entry cycle). This file covers the `0.58.0`
cycle onward. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](../README.md#reference-numbering); chores-12
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: notes/todo restructure (0.58.0)

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

### As-built ladder

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress`
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores
- 0.58.0-5 codify in CLAUDE.md: chores title-only during cycle,
  In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan
  shape for In Progress / chores intros / Todo entries
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

### Outcome

Cycle grew from 3 planned commits (intro trim + Priorities +
bugs split) to 6 as the work surfaced two related concerns:
the bot's routine read cost (handled by extracting
`todo-backlog.md` and the CLAUDE.md `## File reads` rule),
and the chores/In-Progress duplication during cycles (handled
by codifying the new "chores title-only during cycle, move at
close-out" mechanic and the problem+plan shape).

`notes/todo.md` is now sized for cheap routine reads — the
head limit=60 covers intro + `## In Progress` + `## Priorities`,
the live surfaces. The split infrastructure (`notes/bugs.md`,
`notes/todo-backlog.md`, re-packed per-file refs) makes
future similar splits cheap.

# References

bugs split) to 6 as the work surfaced two related concerns:
the bot's routine read cost (handled by extracting
`todo-backlog.md` and the CLAUDE.md `## File reads` rule),
and the chores/In-Progress duplication during cycles (handled
by codifying the new "chores title-only during cycle, move at
close-out" mechanic and the problem+plan shape).

`notes/todo.md` is now sized for cheap routine reads — the
head limit=60 covers intro + `## In Progress` + `## Priorities`,
the live surfaces. The split infrastructure (`notes/bugs.md`,
`notes/todo-backlog.md`, re-packed per-file refs) makes
future similar splits cheap.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**refactor: notes/todo.md restructure + bugs.md split (0.58.0)**

`notes/todo.md` is too large for routine reads and its
`## Todo` intro duplicates `notes/README.md`.

- 0.58.0-0 Preparation (done)
  - backfill `0.57.0` `Commits:` ref in chores-11
  - bump Cargo.toml to `0.58.0-0`
  - open chores-12 with bare section header
  - add minimal `## File reads` to CLAUDE.md
  - populate `## In Progress` (this block)
- 0.58.0-1 add `## Priorities` + trim `## Todo` intro (done)
- 0.58.0-2 move `## Bugs` → `notes/bugs.md`; pointer in todo.md (done)
- 0.58.0-3 extract `notes/todo-backlog.md` (prioritized entries
  stay in todo.md; references re-packed per file) (done)
- 0.58.0-4 expand CLAUDE.md `## File reads` to bugs.md / chores (done)
- 0.58.0-5 codify in CLAUDE.md: chores title-only during cycle,
  In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan
  shape for In Progress / chores intros / Todo entries (done)
- 0.58.0 close-out: move In Progress block into chores-12;
  `## Done` entry; update `notes/README.md`

## Priorities

- P1 is highest priority, same priority are grouped equally
- A line with `...` is a prefix-match against a `## Todo` title
  - resolves locally (in this file); should be unique
- Entries without a prefix-match are free-form
  - chores tasks, ad-hoc reminders.

### P1

- chores-11 is "full" > 1,000 lines, start chores-12.md

### P2

- `**single-field...**`

### P3

- `**por -> dual...**`
- `**por/dual parity...**`

## Todo

 Prioritized entries (referenced by `## Priorities`).
 Full backlog in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Keep
 entries brief — 1-3 lines; detail in
 `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections (link via
 `[N]` ref). Run `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md`
 to renumber.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
3. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[1]]

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]

# References

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
The chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; older ones are closed
(chores-09.md and earlier — through the 0.48.x cycles), and the
active one ([chores-10.md](chores-10.md), the 0.49.0 cycle onward)
also carries the **refactor-tracking** tables (the Context+Params
port and the options_flags extraction).

App architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in chores-10.md) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md is organized into `## In Progress`, `## Todo`, `## Bugs`,
and `## Done` sections; each item is a short description with
reference links to more detail.

`## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` …
numbers in the source so you can grep, count, and reference
them ("let's work on #3"). You don't hand-maintain the
numbers — insert, delete, or reorder entries freely, then
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` renumbers the list and
normalizes continuation-line indent. `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone
only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo` is the read-only check.
The `## Done` section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Reference citations are **double-bracketed** — `[[1]]`, or
`[[2]],[[3]]` for several — so the brackets show when rendered
(a bare `[1]` renders as just `1`, which doesn't read as a
reference). Separate multiple with a comma: `[[2]],[[3]]`, not
`[2,3]` (a single ref key — won't resolve) or `[[2]][[3]]`. The
`[N]:` definition lines and inline `[text](url)` / `[text](#anchor)`
links stay single-bracketed (their brackets already show in
source, and the link text reads as a link when rendered).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [[1]]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [[2]],[[3]]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

Treat `[N]` like a **footnote**: the number is a local slot, only
meaningful within its file's `# References`. So a `[N]` *citation*
(bare `[N]`, or doubled `[[N]]` — see below) never reuses another
file's number; to cite a target a sibling file references, pick
your own next-local slot and define it (the same target may carry
a different number in each file). A `[N]` *inside a code span*
(`` `[72]` ``) is different — that's a quoted identifier: literal
text naming a ref-key (often from another file's namespace), data,
not a citation; it needs no definition here. To point at a section
of another file from prose, use an inline link with an anchor —
`[that section](../chores-07.md#…)` — not a bare number.

A `chores-NN.md` `# References` entry is usually a
`/notes/<file>.md#anchor` (or `/ARCHITECTURE.md`) path, but may
also be a **commit reference** —
`[N]: <commit-url-with-12-hex-SHA> "<full-40-hex-SHA>"`, cited by
a section's `Commits:` line. See
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md#chores-commit-references) for the
why and the exact shape.

A file's `# References` can be **re-packed** to a contiguous
`[1]..[N]` in first-citation-appearance order — walk the file's
prose in document order (`todo.md`: `## Todo` then `## Done`;
`chores-NN.md`: top to bottom) and number refs as their first
`[[N]]` citation appears. This is a file-local rewrite — only
that file's `[[N]]` citations and `[N]:` definitions move; every
target and sibling file is untouched. A `[[N]]` inside a `` ` ``
code span is a literal token, not a citation, and is left alone.
Do it opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
annoy), not on a schedule: `todo.md` fragments fastest (entries
land and get pruned every cycle) and is the usual candidate;
`chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly and only need it
after an unusual event (e.g. a bulk retrofit that allocated slots
out of document order).

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

refactor: notes/todo restructure (0.58.0)

Body:

```
Close-out bookkeeping for the 0.58.0 cycle.

- Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock: version `0.58.0-0` → `0.58.0`
- notes/todo.md
  -- `## In Progress`: cleared (`_No cycle currently in

     "title only holds position" rule

     `## Todo` pointer to todo-backlog.md, `## Bugs` pointer
     to bugs.md

     and `bugs.md`

```

content.text:
Close-out committed. **Post close-out review.**

Cycle landed as 7 commits on top of `0.57.0`:

- `0.58.0-0` Preparation
- `0.58.0-1` Priorities + intro trim
- `0.58.0-2` bugs.md split
- `0.58.0-3` todo-backlog.md split
- `0.58.0-4` CLAUDE.md File reads expansion
- `0.58.0-5` CLAUDE.md protocol codification
- `0.58.0` close-out (bare)

**Shape choices for the publishing push:**

- **Squash** — `main` gets one commit (`refactor: notes/todo restructure (0.58.0)`). Per-step granularity preserved only in the chores `### As-built ladder`. Cost: loses per-commit git history; `git log --grep="todo-backlog"` would only find the squashed commit, not the individual split commit.
- **Merge (non-FF)** — `main` gains one merge commit (`0.58.0`) with parents `<0.57.0>` and the cycle tip `0.58.0-5`; sub-steps stay reachable via the merge's second parent. `git log --first-parent main` reads one jump from `0.57.0` → `0.58.0`. **Same shape as 0.57.0** (recent precedent).
- **Keep separate** — 7 commits land directly on main. Most history; noisiest.

I'd lean **Merge** — the per-step commits are each cohesive (Priorities, bugs split, backlog split, File reads rule, protocol codification) and worth preserving for archaeology; the merge keeps `main --first-parent` reading as one cycle per jump.

Push target: bookmark `main` (the app-repo working branch).

Your choice on shape, and approval to push?

message.content: One one hand I like the merge as you can identify the span of a "multi-step" in gitk and `jj log -r ..@ -n 20` but that implies we're going to make it the default from now on, thoughts on that?

content.text:
Yes, I'd make merge the default — with one carve-out — based on recent practice and the asymmetric tradeoffs.

**Why merge as default works:**

- **Recent practice already converges.** 0.56.0, 0.57.0, and now 0.58.0 all chose merge. Three in a row → the bot is defaulting in conversation; codifying it just makes the default explicit.
- **Merge is the only shape that gives both archaeology AND linearity.** Squash loses sub-step commits (only the As-built ladder remains as evidence). Keep loses linear `main`. Merge keeps both — `git log --first-parent main` reads one jump per cycle; sub-steps reachable via second parent.
- **The "find when X landed" use case wins.** `git log --grep="todo-backlog"` finds the exact `0.58.0-3` commit in merge/keep mode. Under squash it only finds the umbrella commit and you read the body to confirm.

**The carve-out: squash for genuinely-one-logical-change cycles.** A multi-commit cycle whose sub-steps are *intermediate validation points* (not distinct landings) is better squashed — preserves linearity over noise. Example: a refactor that compiles cleanly only at the end, with `-1`/`-2` being intermediate states. The bot should still recommend squash for those.

**What this means for the protocol:**

- Default for multi-commit cycles → merge.
- Squash → explicit opt-in when sub-steps are validation points, not landings.
- Single-commit cycles → the choice is moot (merge of one parent is just the commit).
- Post close-out review still surfaces the choice — but the bot's recommendation default flips from "ask" to "merge unless …".

CLAUDE.md `### Pushing` would gain a one-paragraph default-guidance update. That's a small change, but **doesn't fit in this cycle** (we're at close-out). Two options:

1. **Add to next cycle's Todo** (a new entry to codify "merge as default" in CLAUDE.md `### Pushing`).
2. **One-shot tack-on**: amend the close-out commit (or open a tiny `0.58.1`) — but that's churn for a small text change.

I'd go (1) — natural fit for a fresh cycle. For *this* push, decision still stands: merge?

lastPrompt: One one hand I like the merge as you can identify the span of a "multi-step" in gitk and `jj log -r ..@ -n 20` but that implies we're going to make it the default from now on, thoughts on that?

content.text:
Confirmed on all three points — no action.

1. **Merge + push for 0.58.0** — held until you say go. When push runs: 7 code commits (`-0` through `-5` plus `0.58.0`) all need ochid trailers in the paired `.claude` commit. `vc-x1 push` should emit the multi-line ochid list automatically per the design in [`notes/forks-multi-user.md`](notes/forks-multi-user.md). If it doesn't, fall back to a manual `.claude` describe with the 7 ochid lines before `vc-x1 finalize` runs.

2. **Multi-ochid not forgotten.** Flagging back at you: the 7 code chids are -0, -1, -2, -3, -4, -5, close-out — each of their `ochid: /.claude/pxqmtusnorrp` trailers point at the *same* `.claude` working-copy chid (since the .claude repo commits once per push). The .claude commit's body reverses: 7 `ochid: /<code-chid>` lines, one per code commit.

3. **Squash carve-out + always-≥3 cycle structure** —
   - Every cycle = at least Preparation + 1 Work + Close-out (3 commits min). Even a "single deliverable" gets the -0/-N/close-out scaffolding.
   - Shape (squash / merge / keep) decided at Post close-out review — after the close-out commit, before the publishing push.
   - Default = merge for any multi-commit cycle. Squash = explicit opt-in when sub-steps are intermediate validation points, not distinct landings.
   - Single-commit cycles don't exist under the always-≥3 rule, so the "merge of one parent is just the commit" edge case is moot.

Sound right? When you confirm, I'll proceed to set up the merge (`jj rebase -r <tip 0.58.0> -d <prev 0.57.0> -d <sub-tip 0.58.0-5>`) and run `vc-x1 push main`.

1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
7	about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
8	The chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; older ones are closed
9	(chores-09.md and earlier — through the 0.48.x cycles), and the
10	active one ([chores-10.md](chores-10.md), the 0.49.0 cycle onward)
11	also carries the **refactor-tracking** tables (the Context+Params
12	port and the options_flags extraction).
13	
14	App architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
15	generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
16	Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
17	*live status* is in chores-10.md) — lives at the repo root in
18	[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).
19	
20	In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
21	database to better manage the information, TBD.
22	
23	Examples chore file:
24	```
25	# Chores-01.md
26	 
27	General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
28	more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
29	how and why of a particular chore.
30	
31	## Create a binary that lists jj info 
32	
33	This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
34	and using `jj-lib`
35	```
36	
37	## Workflow and conventions
38	
39	Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
40	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:
41	
42	- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
43	  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
44	- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
45	  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
46	  stuck detection.
47	- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
48	  discipline with hard stop after finalize.
49	
50	## Todo format
51	
52	Todo.md is organized into `## In Progress`, `## Todo`, `## Bugs`,
53	and `## Done` sections; each item is a short description with
54	reference links to more detail.
55	
56	`## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` …
57	numbers in the source so you can grep, count, and reference
58	them ("let's work on #3"). You don't hand-maintain the
59	numbers — insert, delete, or reorder entries freely, then
60	`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` renumbers the list and
61	normalizes continuation-line indent. `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone
62	only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo` is the read-only check.
63	The `## Done` section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
64	referenced by number once completed.
65	
66	Reference citations are **double-bracketed** — `[[1]]`, or
67	`[[2]],[[3]]` for several — so the brackets show when rendered
68	(a bare `[1]` renders as just `1`, which doesn't read as a
69	reference). Separate multiple with a comma: `[[2]],[[3]]`, not
70	`[2,3]` (a single ref key — won't resolve) or `[[2]][[3]]`. The
71	`[N]:` definition lines and inline `[text](url)` / `[text](#anchor)`
72	links stay single-bracketed (their brackets already show in
73	source, and the link text reads as a link when rendered).
74	
75	Examples:
76	
77	# Todo
78	- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
79	- Fix bug Y [[1]]
80	
81	# Done
82	- Fixed issue Z [[2]],[[3]]
83	
84	[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
85	[2]: issues.md#issue-z
86	[3]: fixes.md#fix-z
87	
88	## Reference numbering
89	
90	Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
91	file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
92	are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
93	`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
94	different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.
95	
96	Treat `[N]` like a **footnote**: the number is a local slot, only
97	meaningful within its file's `# References`. So a `[N]` *citation*
98	(bare `[N]`, or doubled `[[N]]` — see below) never reuses another
99	file's number; to cite a target a sibling file references, pick
100	your own next-local slot and define it (the same target may carry
101	a different number in each file). A `[N]` *inside a code span*
102	(`` `[72]` ``) is different — that's a quoted identifier: literal
103	text naming a ref-key (often from another file's namespace), data,
104	not a citation; it needs no definition here. To point at a section
105	of another file from prose, use an inline link with an anchor —
106	`[that section](../chores-07.md#…)` — not a bare number.
107	
108	A `chores-NN.md` `# References` entry is usually a
109	`/notes/<file>.md#anchor` (or `/ARCHITECTURE.md`) path, but may
110	also be a **commit reference** —
111	`[N]: <commit-url-with-12-hex-SHA> "<full-40-hex-SHA>"`, cited by
112	a section's `Commits:` line. See
113	[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md#chores-commit-references) for the
114	why and the exact shape.
115	
116	A file's `# References` can be **re-packed** to a contiguous
117	`[1]..[N]` in first-citation-appearance order — walk the file's
118	prose in document order (`todo.md`: `## Todo` then `## Done`;
119	`chores-NN.md`: top to bottom) and number refs as their first
120	`[[N]]` citation appears. This is a file-local rewrite — only
121	that file's `[[N]]` citations and `[N]:` definitions move; every
122	target and sibling file is untouched. A `[[N]]` inside a `` ` ``
123	code span is a literal token, not a citation, and is left alone.
124	Do it opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
125	annoy), not on a schedule: `todo.md` fragments fastest (entries
126	land and get pruned every cycle) and is the usual candidate;
127	`chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly and only need it
128	after an unusual event (e.g. a bulk retrofit that allocated slots
129	out of document order).
130	
131	## Retiring Done entries
132	
133	`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
134	work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
135	beats:
136	
137	- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
138	  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
139	  context and migrate them.
140	- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
141	  bumping the version.
142	
143	Migration mechanics:
144	
145	- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
146	  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
147	- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
148	  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
149	  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
150	- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
151	  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
152	  frees the numbers for future reuse.

vc-x1 0.58.0

Dual-repo commit+push+finalize in one resumable command.

Collapses today's manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (fmt/clippy/test) → review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-app → commit-claude (skipped if clean) → bookmark-both
→ push-app → finalize-claude. Failures in commit-app /
commit-claude / bookmark-both roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-app.
After push-app succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (positional form of `--bookmark`).
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

          Possible values:
          - preflight:       Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate

content.content:
second gate
          - commit-app:      Commit the app repo
          - commit-claude:   Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if
            empty)
          - bookmark-both:   Advance both bookmarks to `@-`
          - push-app:        `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - finalize-claude: `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push <b>
            ...`

          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-finalize
          Stop before `finalize-claude` so it can be run manually

message.content:
I stopped you as I wasn't sure if `vc-x1 push` included `finalize` or not because we do want to finalize with the 10 sec delay so we get "all" of our conversation. Looking at the help for push it implies there is a finalize as there isa `--no-finalize` bool, but I don't see the `--delay` so I think we should use `vc-x1 finalize` as it does do a push and has --delay instead of `vc-x1 push` but I definitely might be wrong. So let's disucss it and experiment if we don't know!

Thoughts do no actions, please.

lastPrompt: I stopped you as I wasn't sure if `vc-x1 push` included `finalize` or not because we do want to finalize with the 10 sec delay so we get "all" of our conversation. Looking at the help for push it impl…

content.text:
Good catch. Let me lay out what I'm confident about and what's uncertain:

**Confirmed from `vc-x1 push --help`:**
- Push includes finalize as its last stage (`finalize-claude`).
- That stage's hint text says it runs `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push <b> ...` — the `...` hides the exact flags.
- `--no-finalize` exists explicitly to stop push before that stage so finalize can be run manually.

**Uncertain:** Whether push's auto-finalize-claude stage passes `--delay 10 --detach --log` (the CLAUDE.md fallback recipe's flags) by default. The help text doesn't say.

**Two paths:**

1. **Read source** — look at how push invokes finalize-claude. If it already passes `--delay 10 --detach`, push alone is fine. Cheap to check.
2. **`--no-finalize` + manual finalize** — known-good per the CLAUDE.md fallback recipe:
   ```
   vc-x1 push main --no-finalize ... [stops after push-app]
   vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push main --delay 10 --detach --log /tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log
   ```

I'd lean **(1) first** — quick source read, then we know definitively. If push already does the right thing, we use plain `vc-x1 push` and save a manual step. If not, we use (2).

My instinct says push probably uses `--detach` and some delay (otherwise the user always has to manually finalize for session-capture continuity, which would be a poor default), but instinct ≠ knowing.

Want me to read the push source to confirm? Or just go straight to path (2) for certainty?

message.content: read the push source

content.text:
Confirmed by source. `src/push.rs:1270-1285`:

```rust
"push finalize-claude: vc-x1 finalize --repo {claude_arg} --squash --push {bk} --delay 10 --detach"
...
"--delay",  "10",
"--detach",
"--log",    "/tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log",
```

`vc-x1 push` already passes `--delay 10 --detach --log /tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log` to finalize-claude — same as the CLAUDE.md fallback recipe. Session capture continuity is preserved.

So plain `vc-x1 push main` is correct here; `--no-finalize` is only needed if we want to run finalize with different flags.

Ready to run `vc-x1 push main --yes --title "..." --body "..."` (with the .claude commit title/body and the 7-line ochid list). Want me to draft title + body next for your review before running it?

message.content: One other thing, previously when we did the "old" style multi-step we did code and bot and used the same title + body on both the only difference was the ochid, which I believe that "magic" handling of the different ochid's handled by `vc-x1` that magic doesn't handle the "new" Cycle Protocol. Thoughts on handling the that situation?

content.text:
Your recollection matches the source / docs. The diagnosis:

- **`vc-x1 push`'s `--title`/`--body` are shared 1:1** — built for the old symmetric model where each app commit pairs with one bot commit using the same message. The dry-run error confirms `--title`/`--body` are required as the (singular) message.
- **Multi-line ochid is explicitly future work.** CLAUDE.md says "The multi-line `ochid:` list and its fork / multi-user generalization are *designed in* `notes/forks-multi-user.md`" — "designed in", not "implemented in".
- So for our 1:N case (7 app commits + 1 .claude commit), `vc-x1 push`'s commit-claude stage would produce the wrong .claude commit: same title as one app commit, single ochid trailer.

**Practical workaround for this push** — pre-commit `.claude` manually with the right shape, then let push skip commit-claude:

1. `jj commit -R .claude -m "chore: post-push session capture (0.58.0)" -m "<body with 7 ochid lines>"` — create the .claude commit ourselves, properly shaped.
2. `vc-x1 push main --yes --title "..." --body "..."` — push's commit-claude is "skipped if empty" per help text, so this is a no-op. Bookmark-both, push-app, finalize-claude (with `--delay 10 --detach`) still run.

The `--title`/`--body` we pass to vc-x1 push are required for the state machine but should go unused since both repos are clean (app commits already exist, .claude was just committed). Worth dry-running first to confirm that's actually how it behaves.

**Worth a new Todo entry**: teach `vc-x1 push` the 1:N shape — distinct `.claude` message + multi-line ochid emission. Per `notes/forks-multi-user.md` design. This is the cleanly-correct fix; today's workaround is just for now.

Plan:

content.text:
Almost — one snag. The `message` stage runs BEFORE both commit stages and requires `--title`/`--body` (or it opens `$EDITOR`). Per the dry-run error earlier:

```
error: push message: --dry-run given but --title/--body missing and no persisted message
```

So even though commit-claude will skip (clean WC) and commit-app will also be a no-op (empty @), push still wants a message at the `message` stage. The message just goes unused downstream.

Two paths:

1. **Pass placeholder `--title`/`--body`** to satisfy the stage; they're never written anywhere. Hacky but minimal.
2. **`--from commit-app --yes`** to skip preflight, review, message entirely. Cleanest scope-wise, but skips the preflight re-run (cargo fmt/clippy/test — we just ran them at close-out and they passed, so low risk).

I'd lean **(2)** — clean and honest. Stages-skipped reflects the actual reality that there's nothing to commit and nothing to message.

3. `vc-x1 push main --from commit-app --yes`.
4. Stop after finalize launches.

lastPrompt: Hmm, I think it is used because we're "pre-commiting" ".claude" (i.e. bot) but not "." (i.e. code) so we do need --title/--body?

content.text:
You're right — I was wrong. Source confirms: `stage_commit_app` at `src/push.rs:1096` runs `jj commit` *unconditionally*, no skip-if-empty check. So with empty `@`, it would create a phantom empty code commit on top of `qz` (the merge) with the `--title`/`--body` message + an ochid trailer. That's a bug-shaped outcome — not what we want.

This is a sharper instance of "vc-x1 push doesn't handle the new cycle protocol":

- Old model: WC has changes → push commits both sides 1:1 with shared message → finalize.
- New model: code is fully committed *before* push (the cycle ladder); push only needs to advance bookmarks + push app + finalize .claude. There's nothing to commit on either side (after we pre-commit .claude).

**Refined plan:** skip the commit stages entirely with `--from bookmark-both`:

1. Pre-commit `.claude` manually with the proper title + body + 7 ochid lines (`jj commit -R .claude -m "..." -m "..."`).
2. `vc-x1 push main --from bookmark-both --yes` — runs bookmark-both → push-app → finalize-claude only. No preflight (we just ran it at close-out), no review (we did the Post close-out review), no message (none needed), no commit-app phantom, no commit-claude (would've been skipped anyway).
3. Stop after finalize launches.

**Backlog Todo to add** (high-value since it blocks clean dogfooding of the cycle protocol):

> **`vc-x1 push`: support new cycle protocol shape (1:N code↔bot).** Today `vc-x1 push` assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared title/body. With the new cycle protocol the code side is fully committed before push (N commits via merge); the `.claude` side needs one commit with its own message + multi-line `ochid:` listing all N code commits. Teach push to: detect this shape; skip commit-app when code WC is empty; compose a `.claude`-specific message (distinct from any code commit); emit multi-line ochid per the design in `notes/forks-multi-user.md`.

Want me to:
1. Add that Todo entry now (as a separate small commit on top of the merge — would land via the merge if we push it in this cycle, or could just be `0.59.0-0` preparation work).
2. Draft the .claude commit title + body for your review.
3. Then execute.

Or hold the Todo for the 0.59.0 cycle?
